text: Urban vegetable farming is a growing opportunity in Kampala and secondary towns. Start with a small backyard plot (10m×10m) or rented land. Key crops: sukuma wiki (collard greens), spinach, onions, tomatoes, green peppers. Startup costs: seeds (UGX 20,000-50,000), hoe/tools (UGX 30,000), watering can (UGX 15,000), organic fertilizer (UGX 30,000), fencing (UGX 50,000). Total: UGX 150,000-300,000. Harvest cycle: 6-8 weeks for leafy vegetables. Revenue: one 10×10m plot can yield UGX 200,000-400,000 per harvest. Key success factors: consistent water supply, organic pest control, proximity to market.

Mushroom farming is ideal for youth with very limited space. Grow oyster mushrooms in bags in any dark room — even a small rental room. Startup: spawn (UGX 10,000/bag), cotton waste substrate (UGX 5,000/bag), polythene bags (UGX 500 each), spray bottle. Total startup for 50 bags: UGX 200,000-300,000. Harvest: 3-4 cycles per bag over 3 months. Each bag produces 1-2kg mushrooms. 1kg fresh oyster mushrooms sells for UGX 15,000-25,000 in Kampala. Monthly revenue from 50 bags: UGX 400,000-800,000. Hotels and restaurants in Kampala are constant buyers. Low competition compared to other farming.
